Process sequence

Map A Clear Left-To-Right Workflow

Use this pattern for technical process slides that need explicit sequencing, compact labeled panels, and simple chevron separators between each stage.

Stage label

Step one title

  • Replace with the first concrete action or input.
  • Keep each bullet short enough to read at presentation distance.
Optional short note
Stage label

Step two title

  • Use this slide when the process benefits from visible progression.
  • Avoid paragraphs; keep to one or two bullets per panel.
Optional short note
Stage label

Step three title

  • Use the center panel for the most important execution step.
  • Highlight only one panel if emphasis is needed.
Optional short note
Stage label

Step four title

  • Use this slot for evidence, outputs, or review checkpoints.
  • Prefer concrete nouns over abstract summary text.
Optional short note
Stage label

Step five title

  • End with the outcome, decision, or handoff.
  • If a loop exists, describe it in the bottom banner instead of adding a return arrow.
Optional short note
Optional banner: summarize the overall sequence, highlight the iteration logic, or restate the main rule that connects all five steps.
Payoneer
Sequence template
